Patch Set 5: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 5: Code-Review-1

(2 comments)

Patch Set 5:

(1 comment)

Patch Set 5:

Strange. Did you do a clean? Re-run autogen.sh and configure? Are some old lustre RPMs installed?

Patch Set 5:

git clean, autogen.sh, configure, make, make install (I don't think I ever installed any lustre RPMs, I only run make install).

Even stranger, "./LUSTRE_VERSION_GEN" reports the correct version:
2.10.55_121_gf4fa178

But "/sys/fs/lustre/version" insists on returning: 2.9.52_1078_g18ebd8c_dirty

Patch Set 5:

> git clean, autogen.sh, configure, make, make install (I don't think I ever installed any lustre RPMs, I only run make install).

IMO, you should never do 'make install' since it is too hard to clean up from. For development purposes, you can usually copy only a few things and then run lustre/tests/llmount.sh from your git/build directory.

Do you have some old lustre modules that are installed or never unloaded?

Patch Set 5:

> IMO, you should never do 'make install' since it is too hard to clean up from.

My development environment is virtualized, so cleaning up is not an issue. But I agree, not needing 'make install' to run the tests would be better.

> Do you have some old lustre modules that are installed or never unloaded?

I don't think so, I run llmountcleanup.sh without any issue.

Patch Set 5:

I think I found a solution, using "git clean -dxf" instead of "git clean -xf" seems to work.

I also found a fix to be able to run tests without needing to run 'make install' first: LU-10346.

Patch Set 5:

I tried the:

[ $(lustre_version_code $SINGLEMDS) -le $(version_code 2.10.55) ] &&
    skip && return

approach, but this means that with the current version the test will not be run... Any idea on how to be more precise?

Patch Set 5:

Use '-lt' rather than '-le'?

Patch Set 5:

But not all commits of lustre whose version evaluates to 2.10.55 will be able to pass the test, will they? Isn't that a problem too?

Patch Set 5:

> But not all commits of lustre whose version evaluates to 2.10.55 will be able to pass the test, will they? Isn't that a problem too?

No, this is not a problem. We do not do interop testing against old intermediate tags.

parents.subject: LU-8271 nodemap: wait before getting large conf if changed

commit.subject: LU-8626 hsm: expose the number of active hsm requests per type
commit.message:
LU-8626 hsm: expose the number of active hsm requests per type

This patch creates 3 new proc files under the hsm directory:
 - archive_count
 - restore_count
 - remove_count

These should help monitor the coordinator's health and allow
policy engine to adapt their request flow.

Test-Parameters: trivial testlist=sanity-hsm
Signed-off-by: Quentin Bouget <quentin.bouget@cea.fr>
Change-Id: I30c9fb658e8c14a181b094b51408c92df609c3ca
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/30336
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Henri Doreau <henri.doreau@cea.fr>
Reviewed-by: Stephan Thiell <sthiell@stanford.edu>
Reviewed-by: John L. Hammond <john.hammond@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
